summary:
Based on available literature, durvalumab is indeed utilized in the management of certain types of non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC)—specifically stage III unresectable disease where it serves as part of combination regimens involving chemotherapy and radiation therapy. Additionally, it holds regulatory approvals such as being designated for first-line maintenance therapy among selected subsets of patients diagnosed with extensive-stage small cell lung cancer who have undergone initial platinum-based doublet chemotherapy followed by consolidation therapy. Therefore, this confirms both broad applicability across different stages and histological classifications including adenocarcinoma and squamous carcinoma forms of NSCLC while also extending into niche applications like extended remission strategies through prolonged follow-up treatments.\boxed{A}

We now proceed to evaluate if there exists any contradictory data suggesting otherwise regarding potential exclusion from standard practice despite these indications — none were found during review process indicating consistent support throughout multiple sources concerning inclusion criteria associated with durable benefits observed via randomized controlled studies validating effectiveness
